frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: perceptual decisions biased toward stimuli from recent past trials

frameworks.name: visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: VWM resource availability modulates the strength of serial dependence

core_question: Does the availability of visual working memory resources affect serial dependence in perception?
key_assumptions: serial dependence reflects a bias from previously encountered stimuli
VWM load taxes a shared resource that supports trial-to-trial integration
orientation reproduction indexes perceptual decision bias
manipulations: presence vs absence of an additional VWM task on alternating trials
load on previous trial vs load on present trial
key_findings: an additional VWM task altered serial dependence overall
high load on the previous trial reduced serial dependence
high load on the present trial increased serial dependence
these load effects were independent of effects on reproduction precision
interpretations: serial dependence is intimately linked to VWM resources
previous-trial vs present-trial load have dissociable roles in regulating the bias
